title: Rejected by Your Bank? Canada's Alternative Lending Trap
summary: Federal OSFI stress tests push creditworthy self-employed borrowers out of regulated banks and into high-rate B-lenders (7-12%), creating a $401B alternative-lending trap as a wave of 2026 mortgage renewals hits.

title: The Decline of the Luxury Clothing Industry
summary: Aggressive price hikes, market saturation, and unethical practices have eroded the exclusivity that defined luxury fashion, setting the industry up for a historic downturn.

title: The New A&R Playbook: Algorithm Signs the Artist
summary: Streaming and social algorithms (Spotify, TikTok) have displaced the traditional record-label A&R machine as the primary way new artists are discovered and signed.

title: The Fall of Canada's Mid-Tier Retail Stores
summary: Mid-tier department retailers like Hudson's Bay and Sears are collapsing as they fail to keep pace with shifting consumer behaviour, reshaping the Canadian retail landscape.

title: Has the IPL Reached Its Inflection Point?
summary: The Indian Premier League's franchise-based, entertainment-heavy T20 model made it a commercial rocket and cultural phenomenon; the piece asks whether that growth has finally peaked.

title: Beyond AI Multiples: The Value in Boring Businesses
summary: While capital sprints toward AI at ~30x revenue multiples, the piece argues the real overlooked value sits in unglamorous, cash-generative 'boring' businesses.

title: Canada's Unsung Safety Net: The Economics of Firefighting
summary: Argues firefighting has quietly become one of Canada's most effective public services, even as trust in other public institutions erodes.

title: The Psychology of Persuasion: Tobacco and Sports Betting
summary: Draws a direct line between today's sports-betting marketing and the manipulative psychological tactics of 20th-century tobacco advertising.

title: When Imagination Runs Dry: Disney's IP Overload
summary: Disney's reliance on remakes, sequels, and recycled franchises is wearing the magic thin and raising questions about the cost of creative stagnation.

title: McDonald's: A Fall From Grace
summary: Once the undisputed fast-food leader, McDonald's has slid toward a low-quality 'last resort' brand as consumers shift toward healthier, more sustainable options.

title: Intelligent Investments or PR Stunts? The Celebrity-to-VC Pipeline
summary: Examines the wave of celebrities launching venture funds and whether it reflects genuine investing acumen or just brand-building PR.

title: Game Over? The Future of Esports
summary: After surging through the COVID era, the esports industry now faces hard questions about whether its economics are actually sustainable.

title: Reducing U.S. Dependence on Chinese Manufacturing
summary: Despite nearshoring to Mexico and others, the US stays reliant on China for deeply interwoven, specialized supply chains that rivals can't yet replace.

title: Gridlock: F1's Failed Expansion into America
summary: Formula 1's American growth push stumbled on the gap between Netflix popularity and live viewership, weak on-track competition, and a flawed economic model.

title: Changing Skies: Why Budget Airlines Matter
summary: Blocked mergers (JetBlue/Spirit) and low-cost-carrier failures (Lynx Air) threaten the competition that keeps airfares in check for consumers.

title: Revisiting European Energy: Mending a Fractured Market
summary: The Russia-Ukraine war shattered Europe's energy market, forcing a scramble that pushed parts of the EU back toward non-renewables despite climate pressure.

title: Buy Now, Securitize Later: BNPL's Growing Risks
summary: The booming, lightly-regulated Buy Now Pay Later market is accumulating consumer-credit and systemic risks that few are pricing in.

title: The Double-Edged Sword of Foreign Investment
summary: Foreign investment, championed as a savior for developing economies, often carries extractive and cynical dynamics beneath the optimistic rhetoric.

title: Japan's Corporations: A Path to Prosperity
summary: Argues a changing corporate landscape and governance reform could pull Japan back toward the economic prosperity it was once expected to reach.

title: GLP-1: Novo Nordisk's Gain, Denmark's Risk
summary: Denmark's economy has grown dangerously concentrated on Novo Nordisk, whose GLP-1 weight-loss boom drives unsustainable, country-level reliance on one firm.

title: Space: The Misguided Promise of Privatization
summary: A critical take arguing the promise of privatized space is overstated relative to the government foundations and funding the industry still rests on.

title: The Widening Tech Gap: Canada's Tech Future
summary: After the 2021 'Canadian Tech Boom,' structural weaknesses and a pull toward the US in talent and capital threaten the country's tech ecosystem.

title: The Crisis of Greed: Behind the Russia-Ukraine War
summary: Examines how war profiteering and corporate self-interest complicate the moral narrative of corporate 'support' during the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

title: Tesla: The Future of the Electrical Industry
summary: Frames Tesla as the entrepreneur-led bet on the fossil-fuel-to-renewables energy transition and the opportunity climate change has created.

title: Roblox: The Digital Las Vegas
summary: Reframes Roblox not as a game but as a developer platform and virtual economy, with monetization dynamics that resemble a casino.

title: Private Equity: A New Player on the Field
summary: Record PE dry powder is flowing into professional sports-franchise ownership as leagues open up, treating teams as an uncorrelated alternative asset.

title: The Business of Ethical Hacking
summary: As cybercrime grows lucrative, firms increasingly hire ethical hackers to test their systems, reshaping how the security industry is incentivized.

title: The War on Big Tech
summary: Traces the antitrust offensive against Apple, Amazon, Google, and Facebook and how monopolization concerns are reshaping the consumer economy.

title: Catastrophe Bonds: Preparing India for Disaster
summary: Proposes catastrophe bonds and insurance-linked securities as a financial mechanism to prepare India for the climate disasters we may fail to prevent.

title: Cleaning Up Oil and Gas: The Future of ESG
summary: Looks at ESG's role in pushing the oil and gas sector toward cleaner extraction methods amid heavy global reliance on these fuels.

title: African Renaissance: The Dark Side of the Green Revolution
summary: The green-energy transition depends on African mineral extraction, carrying human and economic costs that the clean-energy narrative overlooks.

title: Psychedelics: An Elusive Opportunity
summary: Pre-revenue psychedelics companies command billion-dollar valuations on bets that US decriminalization and medical approval are near.

title: SPACs: The Downfall of the IPO
summary: The record SPAC boom challenged the traditional IPO as the default route to public markets, fueled by retail investors and abundant dry powder.

title: BlackBerry is Back and Here to Stay
summary: After its smartphone collapse, BlackBerry reinvented itself around enterprise software, cybersecurity, and embedded systems by leveraging its security brand.

title: Business Models That Are Out of This World
summary: Charts the space industry's shift from government agencies to commercial players with new philosophies, reusable rockets, and private business models.

title: Bitcoin: A Facilitator of Money Laundering
summary: Argues Bitcoin's pseudonymous, ledger-based design makes it a vehicle for money laundering and illicit finance even as it dominates crypto.

title: A New Era of Sports: The Digital Realm of Gaming
summary: Esports surged in viewership and revenue through COVID lockdowns as traditional sports and entertainment stalled, signaling a structural shift.

title: Tesla and Its Electric Bubble
summary: A skeptical 2020 take questioning whether Tesla's explosive valuation run was a bubble masking serious internal and competitive obstacles.

title: Alternative Credit Analytics: Inclusivity in Banking
summary: Alternative data for credit scoring could extend credit to millions of unbanked and credit-invisible consumers, narrowing racial and economic equity gaps.

title: The Streaming Wars: When Consumers Become the Casualties
summary: As OTT services fragment across competing platforms, the streaming wars push subscription costs up and leave consumers as the casualties.

title: TikTok's Time is Ticking
summary: US national-security concerns over data drove a forced divestiture of TikTok's American operations, with implications well beyond one app.

title: Has the Gig Economy Stopped Calling?
summary: App-driven gig and freelance work exploded on the back of digitization, but the model's momentum and its sustainability for workers are in question.
